name: Securityplus
description_short: Securityplus Federal Credit Union is a Maryland-based not-for-profit credit union founded in 1938, offering member-owned financial services including loans, credit cards, and digital banking with lower rates and fewer fees.
description_long:
Securityplus Federal Credit Union (SPFCU) was established in 1938 as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution serving the Maryland community. As a credit union, it is NCUA-insured and operates under a cooperative model designed to prioritize member benefits over shareholder profits. The organization maintains multiple physical branches in Baltimore and surrounding areas, including specialized locations at government agencies, and provides online digital banking services to members.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer financial products. These include signature loans for general expenses, home equity lines of credit to leverage home value, home loans and mortgages, credit cards with promotional incentives (including current limited-time offers of $150 bonuses and 0% APR periods), and digital banking tools for account management. Members can access services such as fund transfers, wire transfers, check ordering, payment processing, and credit score monitoring. The organization emphasizes accessibility through multiple service channels and appointment scheduling options.

Securityplus distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure, which it explicitly markets as enabling "lower lending rates, higher dividends and more services with less fees" compared to traditional banks. The organization demonstrates commitment to community engagement, diversity and inclusion, fraud protection, and member education through its Prosperity Pulse blog and resource center. Its restricted-access branches at federal agencies (CMS and SSA) suggest specialized relationships and membership pools tied to government employees or beneficiaries.

As a legitimate credit union, SPFCU operates within federal regulatory frameworks and maintains standard consumer protections. However, the website content provided does not include detailed information about specific loan terms, interest rates, fees, membership eligibility requirements, or comparative advantages over other Maryland credit unions. Prospective members should contact the institution directly or review full rate sheets and fee schedules before committing to membership.
services: Digital banking platform with account balance monitoring and fund transfers
Signature loans for general expenses and purchases
Home equity lines of credit secured by home value
Credit cards with rewards and promotional balance transfer offers
Free credit score monitoring and credit report access
Member appointment scheduling at physical branches
pros: Not-for-profit structure legally designed to return surplus earnings to members as higher dividends and lower lending rates
NCUA-insured member accounts, providing federal insurance protection up to $250,000
Multiple physical branch locations in Baltimore area plus specialized access at government agency locations
Full-service digital banking platform allowing account monitoring, transfers, budget tracking, and credit score management
Credit cards with promotional offers (current $150 bonus and 12-month 0% APR on purchases and balance transfers)
Comprehensive product suite including signature loans, home equity lines of credit, mortgages, and credit cards
Member education resources including Prosperity Pulse blog, financial calculators, and how-to guides
cons: Limited geographic footprint—only serves Maryland with physical branches in Baltimore/Owings Mills area, restricting accessibility for out-of-state members
Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APRs, or fee schedules, requiring members to contact branch or login to compare rates
Membership eligibility criteria not clearly stated on homepage; unclear if all Maryland residents qualify or if specific employment/affiliation is required
No information provided about minimum opening deposits, account minimums, or membership fees
Limited digital product information—rates and terms only accessible after applying or logging into member portal
best_for: Maryland residents seeking lower-cost lending through a member-owned cooperative credit union model
Federal employees and Social Security beneficiaries with access to restricted SPFCU branch locations
Homeowners interested in home equity lines of credit or mortgage refinancing with credit union rates
Credit union members prioritizing community-focused banking with transparent not-for-profit operations
diagnosis: Securityplus Federal Credit Union is best suited for Maryland residents who qualify for membership and seek lower lending rates, higher savings returns, and comprehensive financial products through a not-for-profit, member-owned cooperative structure. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Maryland and lack of transparent rate/fee disclosure on the public website, requiring direct member inquiry or application access to compare actual terms.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
